steps.title: Connect the base to the shift
steps.content:
If an exponential function is written as
$$f(x)=a\cdot64^{x/c}$$
then increasing $x$ by $c$ multiplies the function value by $64$:
$$f(x+c)=a\cdot64^{(x+c)/c}=a\cdot64^{x/c}\cdot64$$

steps.title: Look for the displayed coefficient
steps.content:
The form that displays $\frac{1}{c}$ as the coefficient of $x$ should show base $64$ with an exponent of the form $\frac{1}{c}x$. The matching form has:
$$(4^3)^{\frac{1}{8}x}=64^{\frac{1}{8}x}$$

steps.title: Match the required form
steps.content: The expression directly displays the base as $4^3$, which is $64$, and displays $\frac{1}{8}$ as the coefficient of $x$. That is the required $\frac{1}{c}$ form. The answer is B.
